raw_consumer_statement:
As of XX/XX/2024, I had XXXX active CC numbers on my American Express ( AMEX ) account : On XX/XX/XXXX, I called AMEX and cancelled all existing and old credit card numbers on my account and requested XXXX new cards with XXXX new numbers. I requested that no more charges of any kind be allowed to be charged on the old numbers. I was issued XXXX new numbers and XXXX new physical cards. 

On approximately XX/XX/XXXX, I received a notice of a new charge posted to my account in the amount of {$110.00}. This charge showed up on my account as posted to my NEW Credit card number. 

I called Amex and asked them how this charge was allowed to my account on my new CC number. 

The representative replied that if it is a recurring charge then AMEX will provide my new CC number to the vendor. They said this is done for my benefit so that recurring charges would not be rejected when a credit card number is cancelled. That is why this new charge showed up on my bill as being associated with my new card number. 

I told AMEX that I had not given them permission to provide my new card number to anyone. AMEX then cancelled my XXXX active card numbers and issued me another new card. 

I contested the charge, stating that I had cancelled the card numbers and requested that no new charges be allowed. AMEX has refused to refund my money, saying the charge was valid.
